questions.text: 02. He told me he will go.
questions.subject: English

options.text: He told me that he will go.
options.explanation: "He told me he will go." - This is grammatically incorrect for reporting what someone said in the past. We need to use the past tense "would" to indicate his future intention at the time he spoke.

options.text: (B) He told me that he would go.
options.explanation: The correct option is (B) "He told me that he would go."

options.text: (C) He told me that he would gone.
options.explanation: "He told me that he would gone." - This uses the wrong past participle for "go." "Gone" is used for completed actions, while "would go" indicates a future intention in the past.

options.text: (D) He told me that he had gone.
options.explanation: "He told me that he had gone." - This implies he had already gone before telling you, which contradicts the scenario of him telling you about his future plan.

questions.explanation: The correct option is (B) "He told me that he would go."

options.text: (A) She enjoyed himself at the party.

options.text: She enjoyed herself at the party.

options.text: (C) She enjoyed herself on the party.

options.text: (D) She herself enjoyed in the the party.
options.explanation: "She herself enjoyed in the the party." - This is awkward and redundant. "Herself" is already a reflexive pronoun and "in the the" is grammatically incorrect. Simply "herself at the party" is sufficient.

questions.text: 5. I found that he is guilty.
questions.subject: English

options.text: (A) I found that he were guilty.
options.explanation: "I found that he were guilty." - "Were" is not the correct verb form here. We use "were" with plural subjects and in past unreal conditional sentences. However, in this context, "he" is a singular subject, and we are not expressing a hypothetical situation.

options.text: (B) I found that he is a guilty
options.explanation: "I found that he is a guilty." - This option is grammatically incorrect because it does not use the correct form of the verb "is" with the noun "guilty." Additionally, it does not use the past tense, which is required in reported speech.

options.text: I found that he was guilty.
options.explanation: When reporting past events or thoughts, especially those that occurred before the present moment, we often use the past tense to maintain consistency with the time frame. In this case, "found" is in the past tense, so the verb "was" should also be in the past tense to indicate that the person was guilty at the time of the discovery.
